digest.component_or_area: identity keyring reducer + epoch-scoped authorship verification
propositions: Add KEY_ADD and KEY_REVOKE lifecycle events folded by the reducer into an era-scoped keyring, with rotate modeled as revoke+add.
A post-genesis KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE is refused unless signed by a currently-valid key of the identity; the first (genesis) key is accepted TOFU without a signature.
Key validity is scoped to a monotonic keyring epoch derived from canonical merged-log fold order, never from the attacker-controllable data-level timestamp.
A key added at epoch a and revoked at epoch r is valid for [a, r); verify_authorship_at_epoch builds the trust root from only keys whose window contains the event's epoch.
Epoch computation must be snapshot-safe: process_snapshot freezes/restores keyring and keyring_epoch so compacted logs reduce to the same epoch and post-snapshot KEY events get contiguous epochs.
New event types must be registered in both the reducer known-type set and the event_append EVENT_TYPES frozenset, with cache version bump and fsck orphan-type classification.
